title: Spaceflight Principle 55
summary: This entry describes a core principle of realistic space piloting and tactical combat behavior, including maneuvering, awareness, and physics-driven decision-making.
details: Principle 55: In realistic 2D spaceflight, ships obey inertia-dominant motion. Piloting requires balancing thrust vectors, rotational momentum, situational awareness, weapons alignment, thermal load, and collision avoidance. Combat considerations include relative velocity management, firing solutions, predictive targeting, and fuel-efficient trajectory shaping. AI behavior must anticipate motion, plan multi-step thrust arcs, and maintain state coherence under tactical stress.
